entry.title: 入门教程如何将 SAP Digital Boardroom 数字化董事会资产迁移到演示模式（上）
entry.content:
为何使用演示模式？ 

为了简化 SAP Analytics Cloud 资产架构并为我们的客户提供提高效率的工具，我们将 SAP Digital Boardroom 的一些核心功能直接集成到了优化故事体验中。这一改进适用于所有客户。 

根据现有SAP Digital Boardroom客户的反馈，我们希望确保演示模式能够解决一些关键痛点，包括性能问题以及功能一致性。 

有关演示模式的更多信息，请阅读博客：演示模式：在优化体验中集成核心 Digital Boardroom 功能 

本教程将帮助大家深入了解如何将现有的 Digital Boardroom 替换为演示模式。这一过程包括两个步骤： 

  1. 将经典资产（故事或分析应用）迁移到优化故事体验
  2. 利用优化体验中的新功能（例如页面导入和演示配置）构建演示模式下的Digital Boardroom体验 

 

将经典资产（故事或分析应用）迁移到优化故事体验 

演示模式是优化故事体验的扩展功能，这意味着在使用演示模式之前，需要将相关资产迁移到优化故事体验中。       

  • 您可以参考现有的帮助文档，了解如何迁移现有资产的详细信息。 
  • 您可以点击此处查看优化体验的当前限制信息。 
  • 您还可以点击此处查看SAP分析云经典模型和故事迁移的详细信息。 

需要注意的是，从经典故事体验转换为优化故事体验时，会提供两种选项： 

  • 转换：它会将您的经典资产转换为使用优化设计体验。您将能够继续使用相同的故事ID。 

注意：这意味着现有的Digital Boardroom在使用该资产时会处于损坏状态，因为SAP Analytics Cloud 不支持在优化故事体验中使用资产。 

  • 转换+另存为：它会克隆您的经典资产，并将克隆的版本转换为优化设计体验。您的原始故事保留原有的故事ID ，而克隆的版本将拥有一个新的故事ID。 

注意：这意味着原本可以访问该故事的用户将无法访问转换后的版本，且与该故事关联的现有书签也将不可再用。建议将此选项作为迁移原始资产之前的验证步骤使用。

您必须对SAP Digital Boardroom 仪表盘中使用的所有故事重复此过程。然后，我们将打开其中一个故事（建议选择原始SAP Digital Boardroom中页面数量最多的故事），并通过导入其他已转换故事的所有页面或部分页面来构建演示结构。 

 

让我们来看一个示例： 

我们有一个经典SAP Digital Boardroom资产，它使用了一个故事，其结构如下图所示： 

 

在此 SAP Digital Boardroom 资产中使用的故事： 

我们将在这里演示一个故事的迁移过程，我们选择迁移“Demo_15429_BestRunBikes”，因为它较为复杂。 

我们打开故事，并在“保存”菜单中选择“转换为优化设计体验”。 

 系统会首先列出阻碍迁移的功能特性。 

在对阻碍迁移的表格进行了必要的修改之后，我们保存了故事，并选择了“转换为优化设计体验”。 

由于所有阻碍迁移的功能特性都已处理，我们获得了两种选择：原地迁移（in-place migration）或将迁移后的资产保存为一个新故事。我们已经在上面分享了每种方法的优点。在这里，我们选择“转换 + 另存为”，这将帮助我们使用经典SAP Digital Boardroom资产来验证演示结构。 

 

构建演示结构 

导入故事页面 

在“Demo_15429_BestRunBike 优化设计体验”的编辑模式中，我们现在提供了一个新的添加页面工作流选项，称为“导入故事页面”。您也可以通过页面标签栏中的“添加页面”按钮，或者大纲面板中页面部分的“+”号来访问此功能。 

在这里，我们将导入“Demo_15429_Landing_Page 优化设计体验”（已经迁移好的第二个故事）。 

统一故事还允许用户导入隐藏页面，这些页面在目标故事中也会保持隐藏状态。 

此处的“概览”页面在原始故事中是隐藏的。 

导入完成后，SAP Analytics Cloud（SAC）会提供状态更新。 

完成上述流程后，保存故事。 

 

演示配置 

配置演示的选项可以通过大纲面板中的“演示”选项访问，也可以通过编辑故事工具栏（扳手图标）下的“演示设置”访问。 

在演示设置中，设计者可以灵活地将页面归类到“主题”或“子主题”下。“主题”和“子主题”的组合将形成一个树状结构，以指导演示者进行演示。在构建演示时，需要注意以下几点： 

  • 配置演示结构并不是强制性的。我们将“不分层显示”作为演示模式的默认选项。 
  • 演示结构没有根节点，并为设计者提供了一个简单的单通道配置选项，可以通过拖放功能重新排列。 
  • 在任何时候，都可以通过删除所有主题和子主题来恢复为“不分层显示”（此功能在受控版本中不可以使用）。 
  • 在演示结构中，并非必须使用原始故事中的所有页面。未使用的页面将在演示模式下保持隐藏状态。 
  • 我们限制每个页面在演示结构中只能被使用一次。 
  • 您可以选择隐藏“演示结构”中的主题，这将导致该主题下的所有子主题和页面也会被隐藏。此外，在“查看”模式下隐藏的页面在“演示”模式下也会保持隐藏状态。 

  • 我们还提供了一个选项，可以在故事中显示隐藏页面。在故事中隐藏的页面在演示模式下也会保持隐藏状态。此外，如果某个主题或子主题下只有这一个隐藏页面，那么该主题或子主题也会保持隐藏状态。 

  • 在一个演示结构中，每个页面只能被使用一次。 

完成演示结构的配置后，保存故事并点击演示模式（位于右上角）。 

注意：用户可以随时在查看模式和演示模式之间切换显示故事。 

最终效果如下所示： 

 

自定义工具栏位置 

这里的工具栏是浮动的。我们将其设置到屏幕底部，以模仿Digital Boardroom中的操作栏。为此，请导航至“故事设置”（扳手图标）中的“查看时设置”下的“工具栏”。 

选择模式为“演示”。工具栏的默认配置是“浮动”，我们将把它改为“固定”，并选择屏幕位置为“底部”。 

 

“自动显示和隐藏工具栏”功能仅在用户将光标移动到屏幕顶部或底部时才会显示工具栏，具体取决于工具栏的屏幕位置设置。 

 

SAP Analytics Cloud 资源 

关于故事与分析应用程序的统一，更多信息请参阅Jie Deng的博客文章。 

关于演示模式是什么以及如何进一步自定义，更多信息请参阅Tunir Kapil的博客文章。 

 

 

原文链接：How to migrate SAP Digital Boardroom Dashboard to ... - SAP Community  

原作者：Vivek-Varma

entry.title: [Question] How to Copy a Company Code in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
entry.content:
Hello,
I am using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, and I would like to copy an existing company code to create a new one.

In the on-premise version, I found that transaction OBY2 can be used to copy a company code. However, I do not see a similar function in the Public Edition.

Questions

  1. Is there a way to copy a company code in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
  2. If copying is not possible, what is the most efficient method to create a new company code?
    • Setting up via Central Business Configuration (CBC)
    • Efficiently migrating relevant master data (e.g., plants, purchasing organizations)

Environment Information

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

I would appreciate any best practices or official SAP documentation on how to create or copy a company code in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Thank you for your help!

entry.title: Re: SAC Date Calculation in DA
entry.content:
DATEDIFF will only give difference in number of days instead of a specific date.  You should try something like this: 

DATA([d/Measures] = "Expected_Outgoing") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Income", [d/Date] = NEXT(ATTRIBUTE([[d/IL_D_POC_RPD_PMNTMTHD].[p/STLMNT_TIME]), "DAY",[d/Date] ))

entry.title: Re: SAC Date Calculation in DA
entry.content:
@Deb_D 

DATEDIFF might not work. You will have to use NEXT and use ATTRIBUTE to pass the no of days as parameter.

I am assuming you want to copy Income to Expected Outgoing but with an offset on Income date ( read from attribute of payment method)

DATA([d/Measures] = "Expected_Outgoing",[d/Date] = NEXT(ATTRIBUTE([[d/IL_D_POC_RPD_PMNTMTHD].[p/STLMNT_TIME]), "DAY",[d/Date] ))) =RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Income")

May be the above might not work as NEXT might not work with ATTRIBUTE within it.

Then You need

FOREACH [d/Paymentmethod]

@Day=ATTRIBUTE([[d/IL_D_POC_RPD_PMNTMTHD].[p/STLMNT_TIME])

DATA([d/Measures] = "Expected_Outgoing",[d/Date] = NEXT(@Day, "DAY",[d/Date] ))) =RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Income")

ENDFOR

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: The Metadata behind SAP Data Products in SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
@Deb3891 : Yes the article is really technical, even "behind the scenes". If you want to understand better what Data Products itself are, it's not the right article. There are some good general explanations on what Data Products are in the web / literature. In ORD there is a detail page, explaining it more generally, but still related to the ORD context.

If you're looking for a more high-level or end user documentation, it would probably be best to go through the general BDC documentation and learning material.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps for SAP Business One, the next-gen Development Environment
entry.content:
Hi @LRGonzalez, you can find a detailed guide how to setup BTP Destinations for B1 used by SAP Build Code here:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/developing-extensions-for-sap-business-one-and-the-web-client/setting-up-an-sap-btp-destination-for-service-laye

For SAP Build Apps, you may need to add the /b1s/v2 at the end, as well as the properties:

  • AppgyverEnabled:true
  • HTML5.DynamicDestination:true
  • WebIDEEnabled:true

See this video how to do this in SAP Build Apps end-to-end:
https://sapvideo.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/?entry_id=1_9czaadvs

 

 

Best regards,
Darius

entry.title: Re: Error "Serial number already exists for material" whe...
entry.content:
Hi adrakev,

If I understood you correctly, you want to use a serial number that was newly created during a goods receipt as an equipment that can be used e. g. in maintenance processes. You can create a full equipment out of a serial number by clicking the "Equipment view" button in app Change Material Serial Number. Alternatively, you can set up procedure MMSL in the serial number profile customizing that a full equipment for a serial number gets created during serial number creation. 

BR,
Alex

entry.title: Re: [Question] How to Copy a Company Code in SAP S/4HANA...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you cannot directly copy a company code like in the on-premise version (OBY2).Instead, you must create a new company code via Central Business Configuration (CBC). The key steps include:

1. Create a new company code in CBC under Organizational Structure.
2. Manually configure settings like GL accounts, tax codes, and financial parameters.
3. Use migration tools (e.g., SAP Migration Cockpit) to transfer relevant master data (plants, purchasing orgs, etc.).

For official guidance, refer to SAP Help Portal or SAP Best Practices Explorer.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_Best_Practices

https://help.sap.com/docs/

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b249d650b15e4b3d9fc2077ee921abd0/22fc75c2e32b429f8791940933d3961e.html

entry.title: Vorstellung der SAP Academic Board DACH Arbeitsgruppe: "Business Process Management"
entry.content:
Sehr geehrte akademische Community in Deutschland, Österreich und der Schweiz,

nach der Vorstellung der DACH Academic Board Arbeitsgruppe “Technologie” möchte ich Ihnen nun die Arbeitsgruppe “Business Process Management (BPM)”, die von Prof. Dr. Hans-Jürgen Scheruhn, Prof. Dr. Jan Mendling und Prof. Dr. Martin Adam geleitet wird, vorstellen. Die drei Experten haben wir zu ihrer Arbeit, Motivation und den Plänen für die Arbeitsgruppe befragt.

Viel Spaß beim Lesen und gehen Sie gerne bei Nachfragen direkt auf die drei Kollegen zu.

André Biener (Director, SAP University Alliances)

****
André Biener (AB): “Hallo Hans, welche spezifischen Ziele verfolgt Eure Arbeitsgruppe, und wie tragen diese zur Weiterentwicklung von Business Process Management bei?”

Hans-Jürgen Scheruhn (HS):  "Als emeritierter Professor für Wirtschaftsinformatik forsche und lehre ich seit über 30 Jahren im Bereich BPM und Enterprise Architecture Management (EAM) an verschiedenen Hochschulen und Universitäten in aller Welt. (Mehr Details in der SAP Edition hier). Regelmäßig biete ich dazu „Train the Trainer“ (TTT) Workshops online an, z.B. im ersten Halbjahr 2025 am 22. Mai und 11. Juni.

Eine große Herausforderung ist auch zwei Jahre nach der Einbindung von Signavio in die SAP, die aus unserer Sicht immer noch fehlende Integration von BPM und ERP in Forschung und Lehre. Diese wollen wir aktiv angehen und uns dabei auch in neue Formate stärker einbringen, wie z.B. in die SAP Learning Journeys und die SAP Business Process Open Lecture welche die bewährten Angebote der SAP University Competence Center (UCCs) ergänzen können. Dabei setzen wir uns für die Integration von weiteren Werkzeugen wie SAP LeanIX in die ERP-Lehre ein sowie für den Ausbau von Cloud-Nutzungskonzepten für Lernende und Lehrende im Bereich Business Process Management."

Prof. Dr. Hans-Jürgen Scheruhn

AB: “ Eure Arbeitsgruppe konzentriert sich auf Business Process Management. Jan, was ist nach Deiner Meinung der wichtigste Schwerpunkt in diesem Bereich, und warum?”

Jan Mendling (JM): "Geschäftsprozesse werden durch Informationssysteme unterstützt. In vielen Unternehmen finden hierfür ERP-Systeme Anwendung, die prozessbezogene Daten speichern. Eine zentrale Herausforderung in der Lehre besteht darin, die Zusammenhänge zwischen Geschäftsprozessen und den in ERP-Systemen gespeicherten Daten zu vermitteln – eine essenzielle Voraussetzung für die digitale Transformation."

Prof. Dr. Jan Mendling

AB: “Die Digitalisierung hat viele Geschäftsprozesse transformiert. Welche Rolle spielt Business Process Management in der digitalen Transformation für Dich Martin?”

Martin Adam (MA): "Business Process Management ist eine essenzielle Grundlage für die effiziente und automatisierte Gestaltung von Prozessen. Prozessmodelle beschreiben die Anforderungen der Prozesskunden und ermöglichen eine kontinuierliche Verbesserung, sofern auch Verantwortlichkeiten und Prozesskennzahlen klar definiert werden."

Prof. Dr. Martin Adam

AB: “Welche Technologien wie Künstliche Intelligenz oder Automatisierung nutzt Ihr in Euren Forschungsprojekten, um Geschäftsprozesse zu optimieren?”

HS: "In unseren Forschungsprojekten setzen wir verschiedene Technologien zur Optimierung von Geschäftsprozessen ein. Ein weiterer Schwerpunkt liegt auf der Integration Künstlicher Intelligenz im Kontext von ontologiebasierter Abfragen z.B. mit ChatGBT, wie sie unter anderem in einem KI-Vortrag auf dem SAP Transformation Excellence Summit am 7. bis 9. Oktober 2024 in Frankfurt von uns thematisiert wurde."

MA: "Zudem kommen auch Camunda für die Prozessautomatisierung sowie Minitab und SPSS zur Analyse von Prozessdaten in unserer Lehre und Forschung zum Einsatz." 

AB: “Gibt es aktuelle Projekte oder Studien Eurer Arbeitsgruppe, die besonders bahnbrechend oder innovativ sind?”

JM: "An der Humboldt-Universität zu Berlin arbeitet die Forschungsgruppe unter meiner Leitung an einer Reihe innovativer Techniken im Bereich Process Intelligence. Ein besonderer Schwerpunkt liegt auf dem Noreja-Ansatz, der es ermöglicht, komplexe Datenstrukturen von ERP-Systemen für die Prozessanalyse aufzubereiten."

HS: "An der Hochschule Harz wurde eine der Forscherinnen aus meinem Team, welche jetzt bei unsrem Partner msg Group als SAP Consultant tätig ist, mit einer bedeutenden Auszeichnung gewürdigt.  Die Bachelor-Arbeit “Chancen und Risiken der KI im Bereich BPM” wurde mit dem Walter-Gießler-Preis 2024 prämiert und hebt die innovativen Forschungsansätze im Bereich der Künstlichen Intelligenz und deren Zusammenwirken mit dem Business Process Management hervor."

MA: "An unserer Hochschule in Kufstein forschen wir seit Jahren an dem Zusammenspiel von Prozessmanagement und ERP – Systemen im Rahmen der Implementierung von IT – Systemen. Ein weiteres Themengebiet in der Forschung ist die Rolle des „Prozessberater:in“ hinsichtlich der Kompetenzen und Persönlichkeit."

AB: “Wie integriert Ihr die Erkenntnisse aus Eurer Forschung in die Lehrmethoden, und wie profitieren Eure Studierenden davon?”

HS, JM, MA: "Die Erkenntnisse aus unserer Forschung fließen direkt in unsere Lehre ein. Wir binden zusätzlich auch frei zugängliche Angebote wie die SAP Learning Journeys oder Train-The Trainer Workshops ein, welche die bewährten Angebote der SAP University Competence Center (UCCs) ergänzen. Diese unterschiedlichen Formate ermöglichen es unseren Studierenden, praxisnahe Einblicke in aktuelle Entwicklungen und Methoden im Bereich Business Process Management zu erhalten und dadurch wertvolle Kompetenzen für ihre berufliche Zukunft zu erwerben."

AB: “Welche Rolle spielt Business Process Management in der interdisziplinären Lehre und Forschung an Euren akademischen EinrichtungenS

HS: "Business Process Management spielt eine zentrale Rolle in der interdisziplinären Lehre und Forschung und ist z.B. an allen drei Fachbereichen der Hochschule Harz vertreten: Wirtschaftswissenschaften, Verwaltungswissenschaften sowie Automatisierung und Informatik."

JM: "In unserem Team untersuchen wir Prozesse mithilfe von Forschungsmethoden der Informatik, beispielsweise um bessere Process-Mining-Techniken zu entwickeln. Gleichermaßen nutzen wir sozialwissenschaftlichen Forschungsmethoden, um Einblicke in die tatsächliche Nutzung von Prozessmanagement-Ansätzen in der Praxis zu erhalten. Prozessmanagement ist somit eine wichtige Brücke zwischen Informatik und Wirtschaftswissenschaften."

MA: "Da Prozessmanagement eine Querschnittsfunktion im Unternehmen innehat spielen seine Themen in mehreren unserer Studienprogramme und Forschungsaktivitäten eine Rolle, sei es in den Ingenieurswissenschaften, genauso wie im Bereich der Unternehmensführung und der angewandten Informatik."

AB: “Was sind die größten Herausforderungen im Bereich Business Process Management in den nächsten fünf Jahren, und wie plant Eure  Arbeitsgruppe, diese zu bewältigen?”

HS: "Eine der größten Herausforderungen im Bereich BPM in den nächsten fünf Jahren sehen wir insbesondere in der Priorisierung der Prozess-Effektivität vor der –Effizienz z.B. auf der Basis von Customer Journey Prozessmodellen und deren Integration mit BPMN-Modellen. Weiterhin in der nahtlosen Einbindung von Geschäftsprozessen in eine Enterprise-Architektur wie TOGAF oder Archimate oder EOG."

JM: "Ein weiterer zentraler Aspekt ist die Unterstützung von BPM durch Künstliche Intelligenz. Die größte Hürde für viele Anwendungen des Prozessmanagements ist die aufwendige Anbindung von operativen Systemen, bspw. an Process-Mining-Werkzeuge. Wir arbeiten bereits heute daran, diese Anbindung mithilfe von KI weitestgehend zu automatisieren."

MA: "Zudem erfordert die Zukunft des BPM ein stärkeres Denken in abteilungsübergreifenden End-to-End-Prozessen, um Silodenken zu vermeiden und die Zusammenarbeit zwischen Fachbereichen zu verbessern. Ebenso muss der „Management“-Aspekt im Prozessmanagement gestärkt werden, indem die Prozessperformance regelmäßig überwacht und kontinuierliche Verbesserungsmaßnahmen (KVP) abgeleitet sowie umgesetzt werden.

Ein weiteres Ziel besteht darin, administrativen Prozessen und ihren Kennzahlen – wie Durchlaufzeiten und Fehlerraten – denselben Stellenwert einzuräumen wie klassischen Finanzkennzahlen. Darüber hinaus gilt es, ein Gleichgewicht zwischen Prozessstandards und Agilität zu finden, um eine schnelle Anpassungsfähigkeit zu gewährleisten. Schließlich spielen neue Methoden der Prozessdokumentation eine wichtige Rolle, um Akzeptanz und Nutzung in Unternehmen zu erhöhen." 

AB: “Welche Rolle spielen Kooperationen mit Unternehmen oder anderen Universitäten in Eurer Arbeit?”

JM:  "Da Business Process Management ein stark anwendungsbezogenes Fach ist, ist der regelmäßige Austausch mit der Praxis unerlässlich. Eine wichtige Plattform für die internationale Zusammenarbeit bildet zudem das gemeinsame Buch "Fundamentals of BPM", das den Austausch mit mehreren Hundert Dozierenden weltweit fördert."

AB: “Welche zukünftigen Projekte oder Initiativen plant Ihr in Eurer Arbeitsgruppe im DACH Academic Board?”

HS, JM, MA: "Wie erwähnt besteht eine aktuelle Herausforderung darin, die verschiedenen Phasen des BPM-Lebenszyklus anhand einer ERP-Installation praxisnah zu durchlaufen. Um diesem Bedarf gerecht zu werden, planen wir in unserer Arbeitsgruppe die Entwicklung eines durchgängigen Szenarios, das Prozessmodellierung, ERP-Implementierung und Process Mining miteinander verbindet und freuen uns über interessierte Kolleginnen und Kollegen aus DACH. Kommen Sie auf uns zu."

AB: “Das Thema Business Process Management steht auch im Mittelpunkt der SAP Academic Community Conference vom 8.-9. September in Potsdam. Welche Beiträge und Impulse erwartet Ihr von dieser Konferenz für Eure Arbeit?”

HS, JM, MA: "Prozessdaten spielen eine zentrale Rolle für ein modernes Business Process Management in der Lehre. Insbesondere für die Sicherstellung der Durchgängigkeit im BPM-Prozess erwarten wir von der SAP Academic Community Conference 2025 in der DACH Region wertvolle Impulse und einen intensiven erfolgreichen Austausch, der zur Weiterentwicklung beiträgt."

entry.title: Getting 403 forbidden when trying to return with cart via OCI
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We are using S4 hana 

We are trying to set up external purchasing catalogs via Open Catalog Interface. The plan is to make them available to the end users via the My purchase requisitions- New app. The punch out works as expected. The external catalog creates an html form as described in the OCI documentation. 

The form is submited to the HOOK_URL.

SAP then gives a 403 forbidden error. I can only assume that we have missed to get some sort of prerequisit in place. entry.title: Re: Displa G/l account balance - summary/journal entry vi...
entry.content:
In the Display G/L Account Balance - Summary/Journal Entry View app, exported reports are in text format by default.To change them to numerical format for better financial efficiency:

1. Use Excel Formatting: Convert text to numbers using Excel's "Text to Columns" or "VALUE()" function.
2. Adjust SAP Output Settings: Check SAP Fiori export options or use SAP Analysis for Office for better formatting.
3. Use Custom CDS Views: Create a Custom CDS View to export data in the desired format.

For official guidance, refer to SAP Help Portal or SAP Fiori documentation.

https://help.sap.com/docs/

entry.title: Re: Hands-on Tutorial: Machine Learning with SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
@AdrianDBorja Let me try to reach out to our Product Group on the error you are getting.
Just to double check though, the hands-on example in this blog is working for you. Your question is about applying the same concept on your own data?

In the meantime I suggest to simplify. (How many different values do you have in MATNRFORAT by the way?): df_remote.distinct('MATNRFORAT').count()

One way to simplify would be to work with a single time series to begin. Also, currently you have individual dates. For events that occur not very frequently this could be a challenge. Do you need daily forecasts or would monthly also help? You can aggregate the data by month with an approach like this

# Add column that turns the data into the first day of the month
df_rem_prep = df_rem_test.select('*', ("TO_DATE(YEAR(DATEORG) || '-' || MONTH(DATEORG) || '-01')", 'FIRSTDAYOFMONTH'))
# Then aggregate on the month, as basis for a time series forecast
df_rem_prep.agg([('sum', 'KPI', 'KPI_AGG')], group_by='FIRSTDAYOFMONTH').collect()

entry.title: Benefit From SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack Stack 03
entry.content:
To complete the blog series about innovations in SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) coming in SAP S/4HANA 2023 (Initial Stack I Feature Pack Stack 01 I Feature Pack Stack 02), I'd now like to share delta information about the key MDG features coming with Feature Pack Stack 03.

Enabling field extensibility in customer and supplier views (cloud-ready mode)

To increase the effectiveness in master data governance of business-partner data in cloud-ready mode, we have enabled the following field extensibility capabilities: 

  • Apply field extensibility (custom fields) not only in the business-partner core view (BUT000), but also in customer and supplier views.
  • Extend fields in the following views:
    • Customer core view (KNA1)
    • Customer company code view (KNB1)
    • Customer sales area core view (KNVV)
    • Supplier core view (LFA1)
    • Supplier company code view (LFB1)
    • Supplier purchasing org view (LFM1)

Field extensibility in supplier view (cloud-ready mode)

Assignment of data controllers to a business partner in the classic mode of central governance

It is now possible to manually assign one or more data controllers to a business partner and set the "Invalid Assignment Status" for existing data controllers. Additionally, the system can immediately derive data controllers when the corresponding organizational units are maintained.

Setting the "Invalid Assignment Status" for existing data controllers

Enhanced validation of changes in financial data 

To avoid errors in the governance of financial data, checks for the most business-critical attributes can be made with remote function calls to target systems to make sure that proposed changes in MDG are validated before change request activation.

Defining target systems for remote validation and display of validation results in MDG processing

Gen AI-based assisted change for custom objects and summary of mass changes*

To further increase the effectiveness through AI in central governance​, we have enabled AI-assisted changes for reuse entities in custom objects and the AI-based summary of change requests with multiple objects (mass changes).

AI-assisted change and AI-based summary of changes

(* Please note that these Gen AI-based features are only available with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition)

To get the full picture of planned innovations in SAP Master Data Governance across the entire MDG solution portfolio, see the SAP Roadmap Explorer.

To stay up to date, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance and join the Q&A and discussion forum.

I currently see: "This syntax is only supported temporarily in the current ABAP language version and must be replaced with valid syntax for that version [...] This variant should not be used any more. Especially for accessing structure components, other variants are preferable." So, it means, use: ASSIGN COMPONENT OF STRUCTURE.

ABAP Cloud - ABAP Keyword Documentation

entry.title: How to Provide Customizing as a SaaS Provider
entry.content:
Currently, there is no effective way for partners to deliver customizing for their provided SaaS solution. This blog illustrates a programmatic approach to use the Business Configuration Maintenance Object and an Application Job to create, update, and delete customizing entries. This blog should be considered as an alternative approach to the Fiori App Upload Business Configuration. The Upload Business Configuration App  has the limitation, that it does not validate the uploaded customizing data. With this approach described here, you can circumvent this limitation.

This blog covers the following topics:

  1. Create Business Configuration Maintenance Object
  2. Providing Authorization Control for a Business Configuration Maintenance Object
  3. Create an Application Job
  4. Test your Development
  5. Summary

Create Business Configuration Maintenance Object

The Business Configuration Maintenance Object discussed in this blog can be developed using the following tutorial, which guides you in creating a table for storing error codes:

Create Business Configuration Maintenance Object | SAP Tutorials

Providing Authorization Control for a Business Configuration Maintenance Object

In order to use the Business Configuration Maintenance Object, the required authorization must be set up. How to do this is explained in this tutorial:

Providing Authorization Control for a Business Configuration Maintenance Object | SAP Tutorials

Create an Application Job

In this example, an Application Job is used for creating and updating the customizing entries in a SaaS tenant. The customizing entries are delivered within the Application Job's implementation:

 

it_errorcodes_crt = VALUE #(
     ( error_code = '400' ) ( error_code = '401' ) ( error_code = '403' ) )

it_errorcodestext_crt = VALUE #(
     ( langu  = 'D' error_code  = '400' description = 'Felerhafte Anfrage' )          
     ( langu  = 'E' error_code  = '400' description = 'Bad Request' )                 
     ( langu  = 'D' error_code  = '401' description = 'Ungültige Authentifizierung' )
     ( langu  = 'E' error_code  = '401' description = 'Unauthorized' )                
     ( langu  = 'D' error_code  = '403' description = 'Fehlende Berechtigung' )
     ( langu  = 'E' error_code  = '403' description = 'Forbidden' ) )

 

Using the Entity Manipulation Language for Validating the Customizing Entries

The advantage of using EML to create, update, and delete customizing entries is that all validations of the Configuration Maintenance Object are performed. In our example, there is a validation that does not allow error codes above 511 as customizing entries.

Example to Create New Error Codes using EML

 

MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
    ENTITY ErrorCodeAll
    UPDATE
    FIELDS ( TransportRequestID )
    WITH VALUE #( ( SingletonId        = 1
                    TransportRequestID = TransportRequest ) )

ENTITY ErrorCodeAll
    CREATE  BY \_ErrorCode FROM lt_errorcode_create

    MAPPED   DATA(m_errorcodes_crt)
    REPORTED DATA(r_errorcodes_crt)
    FAILED   DATA(f_errorcodes_crt).

MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
    ENTITY ErrorCode
    CREATE BY \_ErrorCodeText FROM lt_errorcodestext_create

    REPORTED DATA(r_errorcodestext_crt)
    FAILED   DATA(f_errorcodestext_crt)
    MAPPED   DATA(m_errorcodestext_crt).

    COMMIT ENTITIES
      BEGIN RESPONSE OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
        FAILED DATA(failed_create)
        REPORTED DATA(reported_create).
    COMMIT ENTITIES END.

 

Example to Update Error Codes using EML

 

MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
    ENTITY ErrorCodeAll
    UPDATE
    FIELDS ( transportrequestid )
    WITH VALUE #( ( singletonid        = 1
                    transportrequestid = transportrequest ) )

ENTITY ErrorCode
    UPDATE FROM lt_errorcodes_update_i

ENTITY ErrorCodeText
    UPDATE FROM lt_errorcodestext_update_i

    REPORTED DATA(r_errorcodes_upd)
    FAILED   DATA(f_errorcodes_upd)
    MAPPED   DATA(m_errorcodes_upd).

    COMMIT ENTITIES
    BEGIN RESPONSE OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
        FAILED DATA(failed_update)
        REPORTED DATA(reported_update).
    COMMIT ENTITIES END.

 

Example to Delete all Error Codes using EML

 

MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
    ENTITY ErrorCodeAll
    UPDATE
    FIELDS ( TransportRequestID )
    WITH VALUE #( ( SingletonId        = 1
                    TransportRequestID = transportrequest ) )

    FAILED DATA(f_delete_tr)
    MAPPED DATA(m_delete_tr)
    REPORTED DATA(r_delete_tr).

MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
    ENTITY ErrorCodeAll
    CREATE FIELDS ( TransportRequestID ) WITH VALUE #( ( TransportRequestID  = transportrequest ) )
    DELETE FROM VALUE #( ( SingletonID = 1 ) )

    FAILED DATA(f_delete)
    MAPPED DATA(m_delete)
    REPORTED DATA(r_delete).

    COMMIT ENTITIES
           BEGIN RESPONSE OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
           FAILED DATA(failed_delete)
           REPORTED DATA(reported_delete).
    COMMIT ENTITIES END.

 

Application Job: Execute Method

 

CLASS zcl_apj DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    INTERFACES z_if_errorcodes.
    DATA transportrequest TYPE trkorr.
    DATA i_delete_all TYPE abap_bool VALUE abap_false.

    INTERFACES if_apj_rt_run .
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.

    METHODS set_errorcodes_for_create
      IMPORTING
        io_apj TYPE REF TO zcl_apj_helper.
    METHODS set_errorcodes_for_update
      IMPORTING
        VALUE(io_apj) TYPE REF TO zcl_apj_helper.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_apj IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD if_apj_rt_run~execute.
    DATA lo_apj TYPE REF TO zcl_apj_helper.

    lo_apj = NEW #( ).

    set_errorcodes_for_create( lo_apj ).
    set_errorcodes_for_update( lo_apj ).

    IF i_delete_all = abap_true.
      lo_apj->delete_errorocodes( transportrequest = transportrequest ).

    ELSE.

      IF lo_apj->get_errorcodes_for_create( ) IS NOT INITIAL.
        lo_apj->create_errorocdes( it_errorcodes_crt     = lo_apj->get_errorcodes_for_create( )
                                   it_errorcodestext_crt = lo_apj->get_errorcodestext_for_create( )
                                   transportrequest      = transportrequest ).
      ENDIF.

      IF lo_apj->get_errorcodes_for_update( ) IS NOT INITIAL.
        lo_apj->update_errorocodes( it_errorcodes_update     = lo_apj->get_errorcodes_for_update( )
                                    it_errorcodestext_update = lo_apj->get_errorcodestext_for_update( )
                                    transportrequest         = transportrequest ).
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD set_errorcodes_for_create.

    io_apj->set_tables_create( it_errorcodes_crt = VALUE #(
     ( error_code = '400' ) ( error_code = '401' ) ( error_code = '403' ) ( error_code = '503' )
     )
     it_errorcodestext_crt    = VALUE #(
     ( langu  = 'D' error_code  = '400' description = 'Felerhafte Anfrage' )
     ( langu  = 'E' error_code  = '400' description = 'Bad Requesd' )
     ( langu  = 'D' error_code  = '401' description = 'Ungültige Authentifizierung' )
     ( langu  = 'E' error_code  = '401' description = 'Unauthorized' )
     ( langu  = 'D' error_code  = '403' description = 'Fehlende Berechtigung' )
     ( langu  = 'E' error_code  = '403' description = 'Forbidden' )
     ) ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD set_errorcodes_for_update.

    io_apj->set_tables_update(
     it_errorcodes_update     = VALUE #(
     ( ErrorCode = '400' )
     )
     it_errorcodestext_update = VALUE #(
     ( langu = 'E' ErrorCode = '400' description = 'Bad Request' )
     ) ) .

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

Used Interface

INTERFACE z_if_errorcodes
  PUBLIC .

  CONSTANTS: log_object    TYPE cl_bali_header_setter=>ty_object VALUE 'Z_ERRORCODES',
             log_subobject TYPE cl_bali_header_setter=>ty_subobject VALUE 'JOB'.

  TYPES:
    tyt_errorocdes            TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zerrorcode_000 WITH KEY error_code,
    tyt_errorcodestext        TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zerrorcodet_000 WITH KEY langu error_code,
    tyt_errorcodes_update     TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE  ZI_ErrorCode,
    tyt_errorcodestext_update TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE  ZI_ErrorCodeText.

ENDINTERFACE.

Business Logic of the Application Job's Execute Method 

 

CLASS zcl_apj_helper DEFINITION
  PUBLIC FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS constructor.

    METHODS delete_errorocodes
      IMPORTING transportrequest TYPE trkorr.

    METHODS create_errorocdes
      IMPORTING it_errorcodes_crt     TYPE z_if_errorcodes=>tyt_errorocdes
                it_errorcodestext_crt TYPE z_if_errorcodes=>tyt_errorcodestext
                TransportRequest      TYPE trkorr.

    METHODS update_errorocodes
      IMPORTING it_errorcodes_update     TYPE z_if_errorcodes=>tyt_errorcodes_update
                it_errorcodestext_update TYPE z_if_errorcodes=>tyt_errorcodestext_update
                TransportRequest         TYPE trkorr.

    METHODS set_tables_create
      IMPORTING it_errorcodes_crt     TYPE z_if_errorcodes=>tyt_errorocdes
                it_errorcodestext_crt TYPE z_if_errorcodes=>tyt_errorcodestext.

    METHODS set_tables_update
      IMPORTING it_errorcodes_update     TYPE z_if_errorcodes=>tyt_errorcodes_update
                it_errorcodestext_update TYPE z_if_errorcodes=>tyt_errorcodestext_update.

    METHODS get_errorcodes_for_create
      RETURNING VALUE(et_errorcodes_crt) TYPE z_if_errorcodes=>tyt_errorocdes.

    METHODS get_errorcodestext_for_create
      RETURNING VALUE(et_errorcodestext_crt) TYPE z_if_errorcodes=>tyt_errorcodestext.

    METHODS get_errorcodes_for_update
      RETURNING VALUE(et_errorcodes_update) TYPE z_if_errorcodes=>tyt_errorcodes_update.

    METHODS get_errorcodestext_for_update
      RETURNING VALUE(et_errorcodestext_update) TYPE z_if_errorcodes=>tyt_errorcodestext_update.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    DATA ia_log                    TYPE REF TO if_bali_log.

    DATA iat_errorcodes_crt        TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zerrorcode_000.
    DATA iat_errorcodestext_crt    TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zerrorcodet_000.

    DATA iat_errorcodes_update     TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE ZI_ErrorCode.
    DATA iat_errorcodestext_update TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE ZI_ErrorCodeText.

ENDCLASS.


CLASS zcl_apj_helper IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD constructor.
    TRY.
        DATA(lo_log) = cl_bali_log=>create_with_header( cl_bali_header_setter=>create(
                                                            object    = z_if_apj=>log_object
                                                            subobject = z_if_apj=>log_subobject ) ).
      CATCH cx_bali_runtime INTO DATA(lx_bali_runtime_fail).
        DATA(lv_bali_runtime_fail) = lx_bali_runtime_fail->get_text( ).
    ENDTRY.
    ia_log = lo_log.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD create_errorocdes.
    DATA lt_errorcode_create   TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE ZI_ErrorCode_S\_ErrorCode.
    TYPES tys_errorcode_create LIKE LINE OF lt_errorcode_create.
    DATA ls_errorcode_create   TYPE tys_errorcode_create.

    DATA lt_errorcodestext_create   TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE ZI_ErrorCode\_ErrorCodeText.
    TYPES tys_errorcodestext_create LIKE LINE OF lt_errorcodestext_create.
    DATA ls_errorcodestext_create   TYPE tys_errorcodestext_create.

    DATA lt_errorcode_crt_message TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF string.
    DATA lv_string_errorcode_crt  TYPE String.

    LOOP AT it_errorcodes_crt INTO DATA(wa_errorcode_crt).

      ls_errorcode_create = VALUE #( SingletonID = 1
                                     %target     = VALUE #( ( ErrorCode          = wa_errorcode_crt-error_code
                                                              %control-ErrorCode = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) ) ).
      APPEND ls_errorcode_create TO lt_errorcode_create.

    ENDLOOP.

    LOOP AT it_errorcodestext_crt INTO DATA(wa_errorcodestext_crt).

      ls_errorcodestext_create = VALUE #(
          %tky-ErrorCode = wa_errorcodestext_crt-error_code
          %target        = VALUE #( ( Langu                = wa_errorcodestext_crt-langu
                                      %control-Langu       = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                      Description          = wa_errorcodestext_crt-description
                                      %control-Description = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) ) ).

      APPEND ls_errorcodestext_create TO lt_errorcodestext_create.

    ENDLOOP.

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
           ENTITY ErrorCodeAll
           UPDATE
           FIELDS ( TransportRequestID )
           WITH VALUE #( ( SingletonId        = 1
                           TransportRequestID = TransportRequest ) )

           ENTITY ErrorCodeAll
           CREATE BY \_ErrorCode FROM lt_errorcode_create

           MAPPED   DATA(m_errorcodes_crt)
           REPORTED DATA(r_errorcodes_crt)
           FAILED   DATA(f_errorcodes_crt).

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
           ENTITY ErrorCode
           CREATE BY \_ErrorCodeText FROM lt_errorcodestext_create

           REPORTED DATA(r_errorcodestext_crt)
           FAILED   DATA(f_errorcodestext_crt)
           MAPPED   DATA(m_errorcodestext_crt).

    COMMIT ENTITIES
           BEGIN RESPONSE OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
           FAILED DATA(failed_create)
           REPORTED DATA(reported_create).
    COMMIT ENTITIES END.


    IF failed_create IS INITIAL.
      LOOP AT m_errorcodes_crt-errorcode INTO DATA(wa_errorcodes_message).
        APPEND wa_errorcodes_message-ErrorCode TO lt_errorcode_crt_message.
      ENDLOOP.

      lv_string_errorcode_crt = concat_lines_of( table = lt_errorcode_crt_message
                                                 sep   = `, ` ).

      MESSAGE ID 'ZCL_ERRORCODES_MSG' TYPE 'S' NUMBER 001 WITH lv_string_errorcode_crt INTO DATA(lv_message) ##NEEDED.
      TRY.
          ia_log->add_item( item = cl_bali_message_setter=>create_from_sy( ) ).
          cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log( log                        = ia_log
                                                     assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true ).
        CATCH cx_bali_runtime INTO DATA(lx_bali_runtime_pass).
          DATA(lv_bali_runtime_pass) = lx_bali_runtime_pass->get_text( ).
      ENDTRY.
    ELSE.

      LOOP AT reported_create-%other INTO DATA(ls_message).
        DATA(ls_text) = ls_message->if_message~get_longtext( ).
      ENDLOOP.

      IF ls_text IS NOT INITIAL.

        TRY.
            DATA(lv_free_text) = cl_bali_free_text_setter=>create( severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error
                                                                   text     = | { ls_text } | ).

            ia_log->add_item( item = lv_free_text ).

            cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log( log                        = ia_log
                                                       assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true ).

          CATCH cx_bali_runtime INTO DATA(lx_bali_runtime_fail).
            DATA(lv_bali_runtime_fail) = lx_bali_runtime_fail->get_text( ).
        ENDTRY.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD delete_errorocodes.
    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
           ENTITY ErrorCodeAll
           UPDATE
           FIELDS ( TransportRequestID )
           WITH VALUE #( ( SingletonId        = 1
                           TransportRequestID = transportrequest ) )

           FAILED DATA(f_delete_tr)
           MAPPED DATA(m_delete_tr)
           REPORTED DATA(r_delete_tr).

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
           ENTITY ErrorCodeAll
           CREATE FIELDS ( TransportRequestID ) WITH VALUE #( ( TransportRequestID  = transportrequest ) )
           DELETE FROM VALUE #( ( SingletonID = 1 ) )

           FAILED DATA(f_delete)
           MAPPED DATA(m_delete)
           REPORTED DATA(r_delete).

    COMMIT ENTITIES
           BEGIN RESPONSE OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
           FAILED DATA(failed_delete)
           REPORTED DATA(reported_delete).
    COMMIT ENTITIES END.


    IF failed_delete IS INITIAL.
      MESSAGE ID 'ZCL_ERRORCODES_MSG' TYPE 'S' NUMBER 003 INTO DATA(lv_message_delete) ##NEEDED.
      TRY.
          ia_log->add_item( item = cl_bali_message_setter=>create_from_sy( ) ).
          cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log( log                        = ia_log
                                                     assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true ).
        CATCH cx_bali_runtime INTO DATA(lx_bali_runtime_pass).
          DATA(lv_bali_runtime_pass) = lx_bali_runtime_pass->get_text( ).
      ENDTRY.

    ELSE.
      LOOP AT reported_delete-%other INTO DATA(ls_message).
        DATA(ls_text) = ls_message->if_message~get_longtext( ).
      ENDLOOP.

      IF ls_text IS NOT INITIAL.

        TRY.
            DATA(lv_free_text) = cl_bali_free_text_setter=>create( severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error
                                                                   text     = | { ls_text } | ).

            ia_log->add_item( item = lv_free_text ).

            cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log( log                        = ia_log
                                                       assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true ).

          CATCH cx_bali_runtime INTO DATA(lx_bali_runtime_fail).
            DATA(lv_bali_runtime_fail) = lx_bali_runtime_fail->get_text( ).
        ENDTRY.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD update_errorocodes.
    DATA lt_errorcodestext_update_i TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE ZI_ErrorCodeText.
    DATA lt_errorcodes_upd_message  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF string.
    DATA lv_string_errorcodes_upd   TYPE String.

    IF it_errorcodes_update IS NOT INITIAL OR it_errorcodestext_update IS NOT INITIAL.
      LOOP AT it_errorcodestext_update INTO DATA(ls_errorcodetext_update).
        ls_errorcodetext_update-%control-Description = if_abap_behv=>mk-on.
        APPEND ls_errorcodetext_update TO lt_errorcodestext_update_i.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode_S
           ENTITY ErrorCodeAll
           UPDATE
           FIELDS ( transportrequestid )
           WITH VALUE #( ( SingletonID        = 1
                           TransportRequestID = transportrequest ) )

           ENTITY ErrorCodeText
           UPDATE FROM lt_errorcodestext_update_i

           REPORTED DATA(r_errorcodes_upd)
           FAILED   DATA(f_errorcodes_upd)
           MAPPED   DATA(m_errorcodes_upd).

    COMMIT ENTITIES
           BEGIN RESPONSE OF ZI_ErrorCode_S

           FAILED DATA(failed_update)
           REPORTED DATA(reported_update).
    COMMIT ENTITIES END.

    IF f_errorcodes_upd IS INITIAL.

      LOOP AT lt_errorcodestext_update_i INTO DATA(wa_errorcodestext_upd_message).
        APPEND wa_errorcodestext_upd_message-ErrorCode TO lt_errorcodes_upd_message.
      ENDLOOP.

      lv_string_errorcodes_upd = concat_lines_of( table = lt_errorcodes_upd_message
                                                  sep   = `, ` ).

      MESSAGE ID 'ZCL_ERRORCODES_MSG' TYPE 'S' NUMBER 002 WITH lv_string_errorcodes_upd INTO DATA(lv_message) ##NEEDED.
      TRY.
          ia_log->add_item( item = cl_bali_message_setter=>create_from_sy( ) ).
          cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log( log                        = ia_log
                                                     assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true ).
        CATCH cx_bali_runtime INTO DATA(lx_bali_runtime_pass).
          DATA(lv_bali_runtime_pass) = lx_bali_runtime_pass->get_text( ).
      ENDTRY.

    ELSE.

      LOOP AT reported_update-%other INTO DATA(ls_message).
        DATA(ls_text) = ls_message->if_message~get_longtext( ).
      ENDLOOP.

      IF ls_text IS NOT INITIAL.

        TRY.
            DATA(lv_free_text) = cl_bali_free_text_setter=>create( severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error
                                                                   text     = | { ls_text } | ).

            ia_log->add_item( item = lv_free_text ).

            cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log( log                        = ia_log
                                                       assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true ).

          CATCH cx_bali_runtime INTO DATA(lx_bali_runtime_fail).
            DATA(lv_bali_runtime_fail) = lx_bali_runtime_fail->get_text( ).
        ENDTRY.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD set_tables_create.
    iat_errorcodes_crt = it_errorcodes_crt.
    iat_errorcodestext_crt = it_errorcodestext_crt.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD set_tables_update.
    iat_errorcodes_update = it_errorcodes_update.
    iat_errorcodestext_update = it_errorcodestext_update.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_errorcodes_for_create.
    et_errorcodes_crt = iat_errorcodes_crt.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_errorcodestext_for_create.
    et_errorcodestext_crt = iat_errorcodestext_crt.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_errorcodestext_for_update.
    et_errorcodestext_update = iat_errorcodestext_update.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_errorcodes_for_update.
    et_errorcodes_update = iat_errorcodes_update.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

Test your Development

Prerequisite

To run the application job, users must have the authorization provided by a Business Catalog. The Business Catalog must have two "Business Catalog IAM App Assignments". One for the Application Job and one for the Business Configuration Maintenance App: 

Additionally, to view the customizing entries in the Custom Business Configurations App, users also need the SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT business role.

Run the Application Job

Open the Application Job App and select "Create" to create a new Application Job:

Choose the Application Job Template:

Maintain the scheduling options and provide a valid transport request. Schedule the Application Job by clicking "Schedule": 

Check the Application Job Log to determine if the error codes haven been inserted and updated correctly:

Users with the assigned business role SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT can now check the Custom Business Configurations App whether the customizing has been inserted and updated correctly:

If incorrect error codes are provided, an error message will show up in the Application Job Log:

Summary

This blog post explains how a SaaS provider can deliver customizing for a SaaS solution by using a generated Business Configuration Maintenance Object. It also covers the use of an application job, utilizing the Entity Manipulation Language to create, update, and delete customizing entries.

entry.title: Re: Leave of Absence - Employee status
entry.content:
Hi @Priya29 

The system construct on LOA and any other transactions on user profile in SF is based on effective date. 

When LOA is initiated from Time Off application then the Employee Status in Job information gets updated as "Unpaid Leave" or "Leave of Absence" as defined in the configuration. 

Even though the "Actual Return Date" is updated to future date in Time off Application, whatever transaction is followed in other modules still system will maintain the Employee Status as "Unpaid Leave" because as per system the "Actual Return Date" is not arrived. 


For example:- If user is in LOA from 01st February 2025 to 31st July 2025 and "Actual Return Date" is updated as 01st August 2025. User's employee status will be "Unpaid Leave" till 31st July 2025, based on the "Actual Return Date" another transaction gets created in Job information as "Return from Leave of Absence" wef 01st August 2025 and there will a change in Employee status as well from "Unpaid Leave" to "Active".

Actual Return date is the only field which will update the employee status when the user in on LOA. Transaction followed in other modules doesn't impact the employee status. 

Thanks

Arun

entry.title: How do I create a relative selection for 'My Country/Region'?
entry.content:
Hi everybody,

we have a report on the C4C object 'assignments' which we want to send out via a broadcast with weblink to a defined set of users. Since the recipients are from different Service Organisations, we want to have a default selection which only shows the relevant assignments for the respective user's Service Organisation. However, since the underlying Data Source 'Service Assignment' does neither have 'Service Organisation' nor 'Sales Organisation' as an available characteristic, I cannot use the relative Selections for Service/Sales Unit. 

We could identify the user's relevant assignments also with the characteristic 'Country/Region', so I'm wondering if there is a possibility to design our own relative selection which then allows to select for 'My Country/Region'.

Thanks,
Michaela

entry.title: Re: SAC Date Calculation in DA
entry.content:
Hi @Deb_D,

Unfortunately, the ATTRIBUTE() function can't be located in the NEXT() function. Advanced formulas do not support it. 

To get your expected result, please refer to the script below.  

 

INTEGER @Day

FOREACH [d/IL_D_POC_RPD_PMNTMTHD]
	@Day = ATTRIBUTE([d/IL_D_POC_RPD_PMNTMTHD].[p/STLMNT_TIME])
	DATA([d/Measures] = "Expected_Outgoing") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Income", [d/Date] = NEXT(@Day, "DAY", [d/Date]))
ENDFOR

 

I hope this is helpful to you, and if I have misunderstood anything, please feel free to reach out.

 

Regards,

Moonjun

entry.title: Re: Transportation Management : Not used freight units
entry.content:
Hello @Peter2408 

If some of the shipments are handled without TM, then they should not be integrated with TM. That can be customized in SPRO: Transportation Management -> Integration -> Logistics Integration -> ...

If the standard configuration is not flexible enough, you can use the BADI_LO_TM_SLS, BADI_LO_TM_SHP BAdIs to implement custom integration logic.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: how to restrict serial numbers at plant level in ewm
entry.content: Use stock based serialization (type C). The stock has the entitled (business partner of the plant) in its business key. The serial numbers are assigned to the stock. During picking you can only choose serial numbers for this stock, so it is definitely the correct plant.

entry.title: Re: Manage outbound deliveries can't select a batch autom...
entry.content:
Hello,

The issue you are experiencing with the "Manage Outbound Deliveries" app where it incorrectly states that a delivery contains a batch, when no batch is assigned, might be related to the app's handling of batch determination or data validation rules.

Here are some steps you might consider to resolve this issue:

1. **Check Configuration for Batch Management**: Make sure that the configuration for batch management is correctly set up in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. This includes ensuring that the materials involved are flagged for batch management and that batch determination is active.

2. **Batch Determination Strategy**: Ensure that a batch determination strategy is maintained for the material in question. Go to the batch determination strategies in the system to confirm that the strategy is set up correctly for the warehouse process.

3. **Check for Updates or Notes**: Review if there are any updates or SAP Notes about the "Manage Outbound Deliveries" app (App ID: F0233A) that address this specific issue. This might include bug fixes or patches that correct this behavior.

4. **Verify Data Sources**: Confirm that the data sources or integrations pulling data into the "Manage Outbound Deliveries" app are configured correctly and do not have any conflicts or outdated information that might cause this error.

5. **Testing in an Isolated Environment**: Try replicating the error in a testing environment to ensure that it is not related to a specific configuration or custom development in your live environment.

Here are the steps and considerations for setting up automatic batch determination:

1. **Material Master Data Configuration**:
- Verify that the material for which you want to perform batch determination is correctly configured in the material master. Ensure that the Batch Management indicator is set.

2. **Batch Determination Procedure**:
- Establish a batch determination procedure in your system, which includes all necessary condition tables, access sequences, and strategy types.

3. **Condition Records for Batch Determination**:
- Maintain condition records for batch determination. This includes defining rules and conditions under which the batch determination occurs during outbound delivery processing.

4. **Configuration in the Outbound Process**:
- Configure the outbound delivery processes to incorporate batch determination. This often involves configuring the delivery item category to trigger automatic batch determination.

5. **Automatic Batch Determination Settings**:
- Ensure that the setting for automatic batch determination is enabled in the relevant delivery document type and warehouse management processes. This might require adjustments in the Customizing settings under Logistics Execution.

6. **Use of Batch Determination in the App**:
- When using the "Manage Outbound Deliveries" app, ensure that your process flow includes steps that trigger batch determination based on your configurations. Check for any user-specific settings that might affect batch assignments.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Unlocking Efficiency: Leveraging SAP BTP Document Management Service in Fiori Applications # 3
entry.content:
INTRODUCTION:

In today's digital landscape,efficient document management is crucial for businesses aiming to streamline operations and enhance productivity.The SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Document Management Service offers a robust solution for managing documents seamlessly within SAP Fiori applications.
In this blog series we will explore different scenarios and find suitable solutions to integrate DMS into SAP

Fiori Applications. This will help consultants to
Blog # 1: Embed SAP BTP Attachment Reuse UI in a Custom Fiori App 
Blog # 2: Embed SAP BTP Attachment Reuse UI in an SAP Fiori Elements application 
Blog # 3: Using SAP BTP DMS in an SAP Business Process Automation and integrate to a Task UI application
Blog # 4: Using SAP BTP DMS API for Custom requirements.

In the previous blog we covered using SAP BTP Attachment Reuse UI in SAP Fiori Elements applications.

In the 3rd installment of the series, we will cover using SAP BTP Attachment Reuse UI in an approval scenario in My Inbox App using Task UI.We will use a simple SAP Build Process Automation(SBPA) Workflow for the purpose.

Prerequisite

1. Set up a Dev Space in Business Application Studio
2. Add Entitlement for following services in your sub account
   a. SAP BTP Document Management Service, Integration Option – Standard (Instance)
   b. SAP BTP Document Management Service, Application Option – Standard (Subscription)
3. Cloud Foundry
4. Set up SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA)
5. Set up SAP Build Workzone, Standard-Edition

Setting Up SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Workzone

SAP Build Process Automation in the SAP Business Technology Platform combines the capabilities of SAP Workflow Management and SAP Intelligent RPA in an easy-to-use, no-code AI-powered experience. It increases organizations’ capacity to drive process automation by empowering business experts to become citizen developers. Leveraging the power of no-code, citizen developers can build, adapt, improve, and innovate business processes with minimum assistance from scarce IT resources.

To access the Applications provided by SBPA, we need to integrate it with SAP Build Workzone.

Follow the tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-configure-workzone.html   to do the setup and integration

Follow the tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-subscribe-booster.html   to set up SBPA in your trial or paid subaccount.

Setting Up Document Management Service and Integrate with SBPA

We have set up SAP BTP DMS in the previous blog

Note down the Repository ID. This will be used in the current blog. If you are using DMS Application option, the Root ID and Folder ID can be retrieved form directly the url. I will be in format of
https://<sub-account-url>/cp.portal/site#user-display?sap-ui-app-id-hint=com.sap.ecm.sdm.user&/documents/v1/open/rep=<RepositoryID>&obj=<ObjectID>

You may also use DMS API to retrieve the details. Please check the link shared earlier.

The next step is to establish connection between SBPA and SAP BTP DMS Service.

The configuration steps are explained on SAP Help https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/configure-attachment-destination-in-sap-btp-subaccount

Follow the instructions to create a destination “sap_process_automation_document_store” in you BTP subaccount and then refer it in SBPA control tower.

Scenario

We are taking a very simple scenario in this blog.A user triggers a Approval process for a Material, which an approver reviews and approves.The trigger form has a simple interface for user to add few basic details and add attachments.

The Approval task will have the details provided by the user along with SAP BTP.

PART#1 : Create a TASK UI Fiori App

The general information on how to create a Task UI for Workflow Approval process is provided at https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-create-sapui5-task-orderapproval.html  

In current blog we will restrict ourselves to the changes that are required to integrate DMS to this application.

Step 1: Create a new Task UI app using the referenced tutorial.

Step 2: Modify the manifest to update the import/export parameters required for the integration to SBPA workflow.When an Attachment object is added to trigger UI, the folder (Object ID) is passed on the SBPA runtime as a string property. This property is of main importance for us for DMS integration.

 

"sap.bpa.task": {
        "_version": "1.0.0",
        "outcomes": [
            {
                "id": "approve",
                "label": "Approve"
            },
            {
                "id": "reject",
                "label": "Reject"
            }
        ],
        "inputs": {
            "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema",
            "title": "input",
            "type": "object",
            "required": [],
            "properties": {
                "Material": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "title": "Material",
                    "description": "Material"
                },
                "Country": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "title": "Country",
                    "description": "Country"
                },
                "MaterialType": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "title": "Material Type",
                    "description": "Material Type"
                },
                "NotesHistory": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "title": "Notes History",
                    "description": "Notes History"
                },
                "AttachmentFolder": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "title": "Attachment Folder",
                    "description": "Attachment Folder"
                }
            }
        },
        "outputs": {
            "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema",
            "title": "output",
            "type": "object",
            "required": [
                "NewNote"
            ],
            "properties": {
                "NewNote": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "title": "New Note",
                    "description": "New Note"
                }
            }
        },
  }

 

Step 3: Update manifest file to use the Reuse UI.

Add the Component usage following under “sap.ui5” section. Repository ID can also be added as a parameter as environment parameter in workflow for each environment and passed on to Task UI along with other parameters.

 

 

"componentUsages": {
            "documentTable": {
                "name": "com.sap.ecm.reuse.documentTable",
                "settings": {
                    "destinationPath": "/comsapecmreuse.comsapecmreusedocumentTable/api",
                    "repositoryId": "<Provide Repository ID>"
                }
            }
        },

 

After Component Usage, add the resource roots attributes.

 

"resourceRoots": {
            "com.sap.ecm.reuse.documentTable": "./../../comsapecmreuse.comsapecmreusedocumentTable/",
            "com.sap.ecm.reuse.admin": "./../comsapecmreuse.comsapecmreuseadmin/"
        }

 

 

The standard Reuse UI comes in 2 variants “documentTable" and “admin”. We are going to use “documentTable” variant for this blog series.

Step 4: Add the component in the xml view

 

<core:ComponentContainer id="sdi-document" usage="documentTable" async="false" manifest="true" componentCreated="onComponentCreated"/>

 

Step 5: Read the Object ID (Folder where SBPA Trigger UI has uploaded documents) to the component settings in control file. For each instance of Workflow this is unique folder that gets generated.

Please note that the attachment ID property has prefix “spa-res:cmis:folderid: ” which needs to be removed to get the real folder (or object ID). You can do it in controller or use script task in workflow to do the conversion.

 

onComponentCreated: function(oEvent){
   let oComp = oEvent.getSource().getComponentInstance();
   let oContextModel = this.getView().getModel("context");
   oContextModel.dataLoaded().then(function(oData){
   let sAttachmentPath = oContextModel.getProperty("/AttachmentFolder")?.replace("spa-res:cmis:folderid:","");
     oComp.setHomeFolderId(sAttachmentPath);
     oComp.requestNavigationAndOpen(oComp.getRepositoryId(), sAttachmentPath);
   }.bind(this));
}

 

Step 6: Update xs-app.json file to include route for DMS Integration API.

 

{
   "source": "^/comsapecmreuse.comsapecmreusedocumentTable/api(.*)$",
   "target": "$1",
   "authenticationType": "xsuaa",
   "service":"com.sap.ecm.reuse",
   "endpoint": "ecmservice"
}

 

Step 7: 

Update MTA file to enable authentication and authorization using client credential between Fiori Application and DMS service. Add Document Management Service, Integration option as a module.

 

- name: test-dms
    parameters:
      service-key: 
        name: sdm-key

 

Add destination configuration for DMS instance using the client credentials.

 

- Name: test-dms
   Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange
   ServiceInstanceName: test-dms
   ServiceKeyName: sdm-key

 

 

Finally, add the DMS instance under resources section.

 

- name: test-dms
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: sdm
    service-name: test-dms
    service-plan: standard

 

Step 8: Deploy your app in cloud Foundry environment. You may choose to use context menu in project or use terminal commands (mbt build & cf deploy)

Step 9: Log-in to SAP BTP Cockpit and find the deployed application under „HTML Applications“ section.

We can’t test the application here as it needs to be embedded into an Approval step in SBPA workflow and then accessed through My Inbox.

PART 2 # : Create an SBPA Workflow

To add a task UI in an SBPA workflow , follow the tutorial @ https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-integrate-sapui5-task-process.html  In this section we will cover the most important steps only. 

Prerequisite:

Create a Workflow and add a Trigger Form to start the workflow. Add input fields and an attachment section.

Step 1: In your process overview , import a form.

Provide App ID and version and import the form.
Refer to manifest file to get the App ID

Confirm the Form has been imported.

Step 2: Open the workflow and add a script task. Format the folder ID.

Step 3: Add a new Approval step and select the Approval Form imported earlier.

 

Map the input parameters. Please note the attachmentFolderPath is used from custom task created in previous step.

Step 4: Release and Deploy the application.

TESTING : 

Step 1: Use the Form link from start event of the Deployed Workflow to trigger a new approval process.
Make sure an attachment is uploaded.

Step 2: Open My Inbox app on your workzone site. Please note using locally embedded "My Inbox" app in SBPA will not work due to approuter limitations.

Access the approval task item to validate the Task UI is embedded and hast he detils along with SAP BTP DMS Reuse UI.

CONCLUSION:  We learned how to embed the standard SAP BTP DMS Reuse UI in a SBPA Approval process using a Task UI.In the final blog of the series we will see how SAP BTP DMS API can be used in CAP based applications for custom scenarios.

entry.title: SuccessFactors – Variable Pay – Bonus Plan with variable Individual percentage
entry.content:
Business case

In most clients’ requirements for bonus calculation, we would find that the target bonus of employees is split between the Business part and the Individual part fairly consistently across the population. For instance, lower level employees would get 20% of their bonus from Business Goals and the other 80% from their Individual Goals, while higher level employees would get 70% of their bonus from their Business Goals and the other 30% from their Individual Goals.

However, in our business case, the client has multiple bonus plans, which represent the target percentage of the Variable Pay bonus, but this target can be split between Business and Individual parts differently according to the job code, department or location of the employee, or a combination of those fields. In order to minimise the number of Bonus plans we needed to define, we came up with a solution involving custom fields that calculate, based on the target % for the employee and the weight of their business goals, the remaining target to be assigned to individual goals.

I am sure there were other, and maybe better, solutions for this, but this blog might help others to think differently about the Variable Pay configuration for “flexible” bonus plans.

Illustration

To illustrate this, let’s take 2 employees who have a 15% target bonus and work in the same company but in 2 different departments.

Employee A works in Department X, and Employee B works in department Y. The bonus plans should look like the following:

Employee A15% Employee B15%
Business Goals10% Business Goals12%
Company Goal5% Company Goal5%
Department X Goal 12.5% Department Y Goal 13%
Department X Goal 22.5% Department Y Goal 22%
   Department Y Goal 32%
Individual Goals5% Individual Goals3%
Individual Goal 12% Individual Goal 11.5%
Individual Goal 23% Individual Goal 21.5%

Note that here the weights of the goals represent the portion of the salary assigned to the goal, not the portion of the bonus target as they usually would.

So, in the Worksheet, the entry for Employee B would look like the screenshot below:

 

Apart from its flexibility, the solution offers higher legibility of the details of the bonus calculation for the employees. It is easier to understand what each goal represents in terms of value and understand how the total value of the payout is calculated.

In the next section, details are given for the steps taken during the configuration of the solution. Apologies in advance for the length and the boredom you might feel by the end of it, but i guess there is no way to make this kind of thing fun. Nonetheless, I hope this helps or inspire someone when faced with unusual requirements from their customer. And Variable Pay implementations are a good place to find such unusual requirements! 

Configuration

To achieve what is shown in the screenshot above, we have in our set up for Variable pay, the following key elements:

  • The basis for the bonus is the salary, not the bonus target
  • Business Goals are assigned to bonus plan with the percentage representing the portion of the salary that Is their target
  • A number of custom fields are used to calculate the correct weight for Individual Goals

1. Bonus Calculation Set Up

The basis for the bonus is defined as the salary, not as the target bonus as would happen in most configurations

 

2. Bonus Plans Set Up

Business Goals

The Business Goals are defined as usual. Note that the Company business Goal is assigned to the program plan name so that all employees who have this goal in their bonus plan get the goal, no matter what data is in their Employee History

Bonus Plan

A single Bonus plan “Up to 15% Bonus” is defined, with the weights of the Individual and Business Section being 100% (so that the basis amount for the section is the 100% salary).

The business goals are then assigned with the weight percentage that correspond to the portion of the salary the employee would get if the goal was achieved at 100%

 

Worksheet Configuration

A number of custom columns are defined in the worksheet, at various levels, and most standard columns are hidden

Business Goal Level – Business Goal Weight

At Business Goal Level, we defined a column that gets the weight of the business goal as set in the bonus plan, in a Percent format, so that it can be used in other levels of the worksheet

Business Goal Level – Business Goal Payout Amount

At Business Goal Level, we defined a column that retrieves the goal payout amount so that it can be used in other levels of the worksheet

Business Section Level – Business Section Weight

At Business Section Level, we defined a column that sums up the business goals weights to determine the total weight of the business section

Business Section Level – Business Section Target Amount

At Business Section Level, we defined a column that calculates what the target amount is for the business section (to be used for display purposes, instead of the standard column that would show a wrong number)

Business Section Level – Business Section Payout Amount

At Business Section Level, we defined a column that sums up the goals payout amounts (to be used for display purposes, instead of the standard column that would show a wrong number)

Assignment Level – Business Section Weight

At Assignment Level, we defined a field that retrieves the weight of the Business Section (to be used in Individual Section Level to determine the weight of the individual section)

Individual Section Level – Total Bonus target Percentage

At Individual Section Level, we defined a field that reads the bonus target percentage of the employee in their Employee History File

Individual Section Level – Individual Section Weight

At Individual Section Level, we defined a field that calculates the weight to be assigned to the business section. This will then be used at Individual Goal level to determine the weight of each goal

Individual Detail Level – Individual Goal Weight

At Individual Detail Level, we defined a field that calculates the weight of each individual goal. This works by reading the weight of the goal that comes from the Individual Goal Plan of the employee, and multiplying this value by the weight if the Individual Section. Note that the Individual Goal Plan is configured to enforce the sum of all goal weights to add up to 100%, so that managers do not have to think about what percentage an employee is entitled to in their Individual bonus, they only have to assign the goals and spread the wights according to goal priority, the Variable Pay calculation will do the rest for them.

Individual Detail Level – Individual Goal Target Amount

At Individual Detail Level, we defined a field that calculates the target amount for each Individual Goal

Individual Detail Level – Individual Goal Payout Percentage

At Individual Detail Level, we defined a field that calculates the payout percentage for each Individual Goal. This is done so that unrated goals are paid out at 0%

Individual Detail Level – Individual Goal Payout Amount

At Individual Detail Level, we defined a field that calculates the payout amount for each Individual Goal.

Assignment Level – Individual Section Payout Amount

At Assignment level, we defined a field that retrieves the payout amount for the Individual Section (to be used at entry level, since the standard field does not calculate as needed)

Assignment Level – Individual Section Payout Target

At Assignment level, we defined a field that retrieves the target amount for the Individual Section (to be used at entry level, since the standard field does not calculate as needed)

entry.title: Re: SAP EBS: Question on Interpretation Algorithm "20 020...
entry.content:
Hi Vassili, 

@vassili_kromm 

I'm afraid I can't really help you with your actual question. 

However, after reading your question, I think it's important to inform you that the posting rules are a rather outdated feature. In an upcoming release in the (far) future, they are going to be deprecated and removed from the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system.
Instead of the posting rules, I'd recommend you to switch to the processing rules. They're  the successors of the posting rules, and more easy to work with. 

Perhaps, you'd like to set up your new client with the processing rules immediately now, so you don't need to switch in the future? Just a recommendation. 🙃

For more information about processing rules, see: 

  • Processing Rules
  • Manage Processing Rules - For Bank Statements
  • How to Create Processing Rules
  • Overview on Automatic Bank Statement Processing

Best regards,

Anita

entry.title: Re: How to Derive the Duration in Years between 2 dates i...
entry.content:
Hi Ankit, 
that is an interesting use case. 
However, it is not fully clear what you want to achieve. If the target is to pick a price condition dependant on the year of a contract, then this is a valid approach. However, there are two observations:

First: the rounding rule of the duration component is defined as 'Round Down'. This means, that if the duration of the subscription start date to the billing cycle start date is below 1 year, then it is rounded down to 0.
So the result is Years = 0 years.
If you expected to get 1 year, then you would need to change it to Round Up.

Second: The years is also used as scale value and quantity. So in your example, you finally calculate the price for a quantity of 0 years, which is very likely 0 INR.
To me it looks like this is redundant. 
With the table lookup the quantity range already considers the calculated Years to identify the price condition.
Then probably you need to use the standard field 'quantity' in the Calculate statement.

Cheers,
Carsten

entry.title: Ariba Network
entry.content:
Ariba Network 为您提供与数千个供应商的单一集成点。同样，它为每个供应商提供与多个大型采购组织的单一集成点。您可以使用 Ariba Network 查找供应商以从其购买产品或服务，并邀请供应商建立贸易关系。在供应商接受邀请后，您可以通过 Ariba Network 向他们发送采购订单。

供应商接收采购订单并退回发票等。每个供应商都有一个 Ariba Network 帐户，可以指定其希望如何接收订单并生成订单确认、发货通知和发票等。

Ariba 为采购订单和发票业务场景提供 Data Mappings between Business application 和 Ariba Network 的 PI 标准交付内容。
